sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
MOUSE on dark gray had no impact. So MOUSE on dark gray only works when the dark gray is in a certain position. Earlier, when the dark gray was at a different position, MOUSE on it toggled its position and changed colors.
Let me think about what I know:
1. MOUSE on dark gray works when dark gray is at certain positions (not at cols 12-26)
2. MOUSE on divider swaps char/divider colors
3. SPACE swaps char/divider colors
4. LEFT/RIGHT move dark gray horizontally
5. ACTION7 shifts divider and dark gray
The dark gray is currently stuck at cols 12-26 (RIGHT is no-op). I need to move it back to a position where MOUSE works.
Let me try LEFT to move the dark gray right, to a position where MOUSE might work again.
Actually, wait. Earlier, MOUSE on dark gray worked when the dark gray was at cols 15-29 (after ACTION7). Let me try LEFT to move the dark gray right to that position.
Currently dark gray is at cols 12-26. LEFT moves right by 3 (after ACTION7). So:
- After 1 LEFT: cols 15-29
- After 2 LEFT: cols 18-32
Let me try 1 LEFT to get to cols 15-29, then try MOUSE.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: MOUSE on dark gray is no-op at current position (cols 12-26). Need to move dark gray to position where MOUSE works. LEFT moves dark gray right by 3. Let me try LEFT then MOUSE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move dark gray right with LEFT, then try MOUSE
action(['LEFT'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 72:
        b = n['boundary']
        print(f"Dark gray: cols {min([p[1] for p in b])}-{max([p[1] for p in b])}")
        break

# Now try MOUSE on dark gray
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 23, 'col': 23}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 72:
        b = n['boundary']
        print(f"After MOUSE: Dark gray cols {min([p[1] for p in b])}-{max([p[1] for p in b])}")
        break
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'B' and n['pixels'] == 64:
        print(f"Char children colors: {[seg[c]['color'] for c in n['children']]}")
        break
print(f"Result: {last_action_result}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
